question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConstructionMetadata()] Can an unprivileged user inject arbitrary key-value pairs into the options map passed to ConstructionMetadata() that are cloned into metadata via maps.Clone(), causing the mirror node to receive unexpected metadata fields that corrupt or override legitimate record fields?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getRandomNodeAccountId()] Can an unprivileged user trigger a scenario where getRandomNodeAccountId() falls back to nodeAccountIds[0] due to rand.Int() failure, causing all transactions to be routed to the same node, which if that node is slow or faulty, results in missing mirror node records for submitted transactions?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: transactionSetNodeAccountId()] Can an unprivileged user supply a metadataKeyNodeAccountId that points to a non-existent or decommissioned node account, causing the transaction to be submitted to a node that does not export records to the mirror node, resulting in a missing record?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getTransactionNodeAccountId()] Can an unprivileged user supply a metadataKeyNodeAccountId string that parses successfully via hiero.AccountIDFromString() but refers to a node outside the current network, causing the transaction to be submitted to a node that does not propagate records to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConstructionPayloads()] Can an unprivileged user supply operations with zero amounts in getOperationSlice() that pass validation but cause the constructed transaction to have no net value transfer, resulting in the mirror node recording an empty or misleading transfer record?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConstructionPayloads()] Can an unprivileged user supply a metadataKeyAccountMap that maps the payer alias to a different account than the one that actually signed, causing the mirror node to record the transaction under an incorrect payer account ID?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConstructionPayloads()] Can an unprivileged user supply a validStartNanos in the past (but within valid duration) so that transactionSetTransactionId() creates a transaction ID with a past timestamp, causing the mirror node to record the transaction with a timestamp that predates its actual submission?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConstructionPayloads()] Can an unprivileged user supply a validStartNanos that is identical to a previously submitted transaction's start nanos for the same payer, causing a duplicate transaction ID that the mirror node either rejects (missing record) or records as a duplicate (incorrect record)?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: transactionSetValidDuration()] Can an unprivileged user supply validDurationSeconds=1 (minimum non-zero value that passes isValidTransactionValidDuration()) so that the transaction window is too short for the mirror node to ingest it before expiry, resulting in a missing record?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: isValidTransactionValidDuration()] Can an unprivileged user supply validDurationSeconds=180 (maxValidDurationSeconds) combined with a validStartNanos far in the future, causing the transaction to be accepted by ConstructionPayloads() but submitted before it becomes valid, resulting in the mirror node recording it with an incorrect or future timestamp?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConstructionHash()] Can an unprivileged user call ConstructionHash() on a transaction where TransactionGetTransactionHash() returns a hash that does not match the hash the mirror node will use to index the record, causing a hash mismatch and making the record unfindable?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConstructionHash()] Can an unprivileged user supply a signed transaction to ConstructionHash() that has been modified after signing (but before submission), causing the returned hash to differ from the hash of the actually submitted transaction, leading to an incorrect hash in the mirror node record lookup?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConstructionSubmit()] Can an unprivileged user call ConstructionSubmit() with a transaction where TransactionGetTransactionID() returns an empty or zero transaction ID (the error is silently ignored with '_'), causing the mirror node to record the transaction under an incorrect or missing transaction ID?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConstructionSubmit()] Can an unprivileged user exploit the fact that TransactionExecute() success does not guarantee mirror node record creation, by submitting a transaction that succeeds at consensus but whose record is never exported to the mirror node due to a node-specific export failure, resulting in a missing record?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConstructionSubmit()] Can an unprivileged user submit a transaction via ConstructionSubmit() and then immediately query the mirror node before the record is exported, exploiting the lack of any confirmation wait in the code, to falsely conclude the record is missing and trigger duplicate submissions that create conflicting records?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConstructionSubmit()] Can an unprivileged user craft a transaction where transaction.GetNodeAccountIDs()[0] returns a node that is not part of the mirror node's tracked network, causing the transaction to be submitted to an untracked node and the record to be missing from the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getOperationSlice()] Can an unprivileged user supply operations with negative amounts that pass types.NewAmount() validation, causing the constructed transaction to encode a negative transfer that the mirror node records incorrectly or fails to export?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getOperationSlice()] Can an unprivileged user supply an operation with a nil Account field so that accountId remains zero-valued, and the constructed transaction records a transfer from/to the zero account, causing the mirror node to export an incorrect record with a missing sender or receiver?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getOperationSlice()] Can an unprivileged user supply operations with mismatched types (e.g., mixing CRYPTOTRANSFER and CRYPTOCREATEACCOUNT) that pass getOperationSlice() individually but cause the transactionHandler.Construct() to build an incorrect transaction, leading to the mirror node recording an unexpected transaction type?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConstructionPreprocess()] Can an unprivileged user supply operations where signers[0] (the payer) has an alias, causing optionKeyAccountAliases to be set, but then supply a different payer in ConstructionPayloads(), causing the mirror node to record the transaction under a different payer than what was preprocessed?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConstructionPreprocess()] Can an unprivileged user supply request.Metadata with arbitrary keys that are copied into options in ConstructionPreprocess(), causing those keys to propagate through the construction pipeline and override legitimate metadata fields, resulting in incorrect records exported to the mirror node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: ConstructionPreprocess()] Can an unprivileged user supply operations[0].Type as an empty string so that options[optionKeyOperationType] is set to '', causing ConstructionMetadata() to return an incorrect or default fee that leads to a transaction failing at consensus and missing from mirror node records?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: transactionSetTransactionId()] Can an unprivileged user supply a validStartNanos that is exactly at the boundary of int64 max value, causing time.Unix(0, validStartNanos) to overflow and produce an incorrect transaction ID timestamp that the mirror node records incorrectly?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getIntMetadataValue()] Can an unprivileged user supply a metadataKeyValidStartNanos string value of '0' so that getIntMetadataValue() returns 0 without error, causing transactionSetTransactionId() to call TransactionIDGenerate() instead of using the supplied start nanos, resulting in an unpredictable transaction ID that the mirror node records under an unexpected value?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/construction_service.go -> Scope: Medium: Incorrect or missing records exported to mirror nodes'] [Function: getIntMetadataValue()] Can an unprivileged user supply a metadata value that is not a string type (e.g., a float64 from JSON deserialization) for metadataKeyValidStartNanos, causing getIntMetadataValue() to return ErrInvalidArgument, but the caller silently uses a zero value, leading to an incorrect transaction ID in the mirror node record?
